summary: Frontend-only verification of Butler AI onboarding overlay flow on Expo SDK 53 web preview. Visual evidence (3 screenshots) confirms: (a) fresh boot with cleared localStorage shows the onboarding Modal overlay rendering Screen 1 'WELCOME 1/10' with BUTLER AI logo and LOCAL AI / ZERO CLOUD / HMAC-SHA256 / LAN ONLY pills; (b) seeding @butler_onboarding_done_v2='1' before reload lands the user on the NEXUS COMMAND CENTER home tab with the 6-tab bar (HOME/SCRIPTS/BUTLER/TERMINAL/KB/SETTINGS) and NO modal; (c) persistence holds across multiple reloads; (d) tab taps do not crash. Two automated tests (2 & 3) reported FAIL purely as false negatives because the home screen contains a 'GET STARTED' / 'PAIR YOUR PC' button whose text matched the onboarding substring detector — screenshots prove the modal is correctly dismissed. One automated test (4: Screen1->Screen2 advance) could not be confirmed via DOM because the `.first` text match for 'GET STARTED' is ambiguous when both layers render. Core E1 requirements verified visually. Deprecated package detected: expo-av imports may exist in services (not directly observed in reviewed files, flagging for E1 sweep). Misplaced route file detected: /app/frontend/app/onboardingExit.ts triggers an Expo Router warning every render: 'Route ./onboardingExit.ts is missing the required default export.'

ui_bugs.issue: File lives under /app/frontend/app/ which Expo Router treats as a route, but it has no default React component export. Console emits 'Route ./onboardingExit.ts is missing the required default export.' on every render. Move it to /app/frontend/utils/ or /app/frontend/constants/.

Home tab body shows ~28 rows of literal 'Invalid Date' under what appears to be SIGMA-NET / VECTORS INDEXED. Likely a date-formatter receiving null/undefined timestamps. Reference: app/(tabs)/nexushome.tsx

integration_issues.issue: Cannot uniquely target the onboarding 'GET STARTED' button via text — same string appears on the underlying home tab's 'PAIR YOUR PC' card, so playwright `.first` picks the wrong one. Add a stable testID (e.g. testID='onboarding-screen1-next') to the Screen 1 primary button to make Play Store regression automation reliable.

issues: Console warnings on every web render: 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow.' and 'textShadow* style props are deprecated. Use textShadow.' — Expo SDK 53 / RN 0.76+ deprecation. Migrate textShadowColor/textShadowOffset/textShadowRadius and shadow* props to the unified boxShadow/textShadow syntax.
'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — move pointerEvents='none' on the HUDGridBG <View> into the style prop.

react_native_issues.issue: Web console: 'Animated: useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' Cosmetic on web preview, but the warning floods the console. Reference: app/welcome.tsx and app/_layout.tsx — NexusSplash & Screen1Welcome animations pass useNativeDriver: true.
app/onboardingExit.ts is treated as a route by Expo Router but lacks a default export. Move out of /app/. Reference: app/onboardingExit.ts

action_items: Add testID='onboarding-screen1-next' (and similar for all 10 screens' primary buttons) on welcome.tsx — required for Play Store regression automation.
Move /app/frontend/app/onboardingExit.ts out of the app/ directory (e.g. /app/frontend/utils/onboardingExit.ts) to eliminate the Expo Router missing-default-export warning.
Migrate deprecated shadow*/textShadow* style props in welcome.tsx and _layout.tsx to boxShadow/textShadow (SDK 53 deprecation).
Move pointerEvents='none' from prop to style on HUDGridBG <View> in welcome.tsx.
Investigate 'Invalid Date' rows on nexushome.tsx vectors list (low priority, cosmetic on empty PC).
Optional: gate animations on Platform.OS !== 'web' or accept the JS fallback warning — cosmetic only.
critical_code_review_comments: welcome.tsx is very large (>1000 lines). Consider splitting Screen1..Screen10 into separate files under app/onboarding/ to stay under the 700-line guideline.
_layout.tsx uses globals (global.__onboardingComplete, global.__setNeedsOnboarding). Functional but fragile; consider replacing with a typed context.

Onboarding-done verification in _layout.tsx is solid: multiSet + verification read + single-key rewrite fallback + 8s timeout guard + AppState foreground re-check. Good defense-in-depth for Play Store reviewers.
Tab layout (app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx) correctly registers all 6 visible tabs and hides 5 routes via href: null.

success_rate.frontend: Visual: 4/4 critical flows verified (fresh boot shows Screen 1, seeded flag shows home, persistence, no crash on tab tap). Automated: 2/5 strict text-match (3 false-negatives due to ambiguous 'GET STARTED' text overlap).

seed_data_creation: Seeded 7 AsyncStorage keys via localStorage (@butler_onboarding_done_v2, @butler_consent_v2, @butler_terms_accepted_v1, @butler_privacy_accepted_v1, @butler_age_confirmed_v1, @butler_lan_consent_v1, @butler_server_privacy_accepted_v1) to simulate post-onboarding state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After E1 adds testIDs to onboarding screen buttons (onboarding-screen{N}-next, onboarding-screen10-launch, etc.), re-run the 10-screen walk-through using testID selectors instead of text. Note that the underlying home tab is always mounted under the Modal — DOM text searches will see both layers. The bundle takes ~12-15s to render on first load; use waitForTimeout(15000) after reload. Mobile viewport 375x667.
rca_of_issue: Original Play Store blocker (Screen 10 → home transition stuck) — refactor to Modal overlay on top of always-mounted (tabs) navigator is correctly implemented. Modal visibility binds to `needsOnboarding === true && splashDone`. Screen 10 LAUNCH BUTLER AI calls handleOnboardingComplete which (a) multiSets 7 keys, (b) verifies ONBOARDING_DONE_KEY landed and rewrites if not, (c) calls setNeedsOnboarding(false) which causes Modal to unmount with fade animation, instantly revealing the already-mounted tabs underneath. No router race possible. 8s AsyncStorage timeout in _layout.tsx ensures the user is never stuck if storage hangs. AppState 'active' re-check guards against backup-restored stale state. Implementation is robust for Play Store inspection.
